channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on why so many Greenlanders are Lutheran
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — About 90% of the 57,000 Greenlanders identify as Inuit and the vast majority of them belong to the Lutheran Church today, more than 300 years after a Danish missionary brought that branch of Christianity to the world’s largest island.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson slams Dem Rep. Green's 'egregious behavior' during Trump's address
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson condemned Rep. Al Green for protesting during President Donald Trump's speech and accused the Texas lawmaker of engaging in "shameful and egregious behavior.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP rep says she'll refer sanctuary city mayors for criminal prosecution
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna, R-Fla., said she is referring the Democrat sanctuary city mayors who testified before Congress on Wednesday to the Justice Department for criminal prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump's economic disruptions moving markets downward
item.description: Anxiety has returned to Wall Street with markets poised to give back much of Wednesday’s gains, which were spurred by President Donald Trump’s one-month exemption for U.S. automakers on his 25% tariffs for Mexican and Canadian imports.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal appeals court sides with Trump on firing head of watchdog agency
item.description: President Trump got a victory in federal appeals court Wednesday when a judge cleared the way for him to fire Hampton Dellinger.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: On schools, Trump talks DEI and trans athletes, not test scores or student loans
item.description: President Trump barely mentioned U.S. schools in his address to Congress on Tuesday night — and when he did, he was really talking about other things.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tariff tumult gives Senate GOP whiplash
item.description: President Trump’s continuous back-and-forth on tariffs is taking a toll on Republican lawmakers who admit the constant announcements are difficult to follow even as they try to assuage concerns from constituents. Trump’s six weeks in the White House have been a roller coaster on a number of fronts.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican medical manufacturers warn tariffs will increase US health care costs
item.description: "We are worried about patients in hospitals because they are the ones who will be affected the most," said Alfredo Dueñez, president of Baja California's Medical Device Cluster.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Use of military aircraft for deportations has stopped, source says
item.description: A Defense Department source says the Pentagon has not received a request for military aircraft since Saturday, March 1.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Divided Supreme Court rejects Trump bid to freeze nearly $2 billion in foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sharply divided Supreme Court on Wednesday rejected the Trump administration's bid to freeze nearly $2 billion in foreign aid, but it was not clear how quickly money might start flowing.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jobs lost in every state and lifesaving cures not discovered: Possible impacts of research cuts
item.description: Ripple effects of the Trump administration's crackdown on U.S. medical research promise to reach every corner of America.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Kasturi Pananjady, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs, according to internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning a reorganization that includes cutting over 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care and other services for millions of veterans, according to an internal memo obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers. Worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Divided SCOTUS rejects Trump administration's push to rebuke judge over foreign aid freeze
item.description: By a 5-4 vote Wednesday, the Supreme Court told U.S. District Judge Amir Ali to clarify his earlier order that required the Republican administration to release nearly $2 billion in aid for work that had already been done.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota farmers fret about ‘chaos and uncertainty’ following latest Trump tariffs
item.description: Minnesota producers say they’re worried about the impact that tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico coupled with retaliatory tariffs could have on the agriculture industry.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats disrupt, protest and wear pained expressions for Trump’s speech
item.description: House Democrats greeted President Trump’s return to Congress on Tuesday with a spirited series of disruptive protests — and a number of painful looks as they suffered through an address in which the returning president repeatedly cajoled and mocked them.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Fired federal workers are among the guests invited to Trump's speech to Congress
item.description: The Trumps and lawmakers from across the aisle have invited guests to Tuesday's joint address, representing some of the administration's top priorities. Here's a look at some of the names on the list.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: How Democrats will respond to Trump's Congress address
item.description: The administration says the theme will be the renewal of the American dream and comes amid sweeping cuts to the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Latex glove ban proposed in Nevada to help allergy sufferers
item.description: An allergy to latex can come with a lot of limits on where you can go and what you can do. Some clothing, medical procedures, and even visits to restaurants become potential problems.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada's Cortez Masto slams Trump administration cuts amid bird flu response
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto criticized the Trump administration Thursday, saying recent terminations are worsening the federal government’s response to the bird flu outbreak and the rise price of eggs.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
